document.title: IEC 60601-1:2005
document.documentType: International medical electrical equipment safety standard

sourceSummary.oneLine: IEC 60601-1:2005 clauses 8.5 through 8.7 define requirements and tests for means of protection, patient connection separation, defibrillation-proof applied parts, protective earthing, and leakage currents.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Use for medical electrical equipment safety compliance evidence covering electric shock protection, patient leakage current limits, protective earth impedance, and defibrillation-proof applied part verification.
keyPoints: Means of protection are categorized as patient protection or operator protection depending on which equipment parts they protect.
F-type applied parts require separation equivalent to one means of patient protection and patient leakage current testing at 110% maximum mains voltage.
Defibrillation-proof applied parts must prevent hazardous energies above 1 V peak on specified parts during defibrillator discharge and preserve basic safety and essential performance afterward.
Protective earth connections have defined impedance limits and current-carrying verification tests.
Leakage current and patient auxiliary current limits are specified for normal condition, single fault condition, and special test conditions.

entities.name: IEC
entities.evidence: Page headers identify the document as "60601-1 © IEC:2005".

entities.name: ME EQUIPMENT
entities.evidence: Requirements apply to ME EQUIPMENT throughout clauses 8.5 to 8.7, including protective earthing and leakage current tests.

entities.name: MANUFACTURER
entities.evidence: The manufacturer defines whether multiple functions are one applied part or multiple applied parts, and must demonstrate reliability for moving-part protective earth connections.

entities.name: OPERATOR
entities.evidence: Means of operator protection and operator-accessible potential equalization terminal requirements are specified.

entities.name: PATIENT
entities.evidence: Patient connections, patient leakage currents, and patient auxiliary currents are repeatedly regulated in clauses 8.5.2 and 8.7.

concepts.name: Means of Patient Protection
concepts.evidence: A means of protection is a means of patient protection if it protects applied parts or parts identified under 4.6 as needing applied-part requirements.

concepts.name: Means of Operator Protection
concepts.evidence: If a means of protection does not protect applied parts or equivalent parts, it is a means of operator protection.

concepts.name: F-type applied part separation
concepts.evidence: Patient connections of any F-type applied part must be separated from all other parts by means equivalent to one means of patient protection.

concepts.name: Defibrillation-proof applied part
concepts.evidence: The classification defibrillation-proof applied part applies to the whole of one applied part.

concepts.name: Protective earth terminal
concepts.evidence: The protective earth terminal must be suitable for connection to an external protective earthing system.

concepts.name: Leakage current limits
concepts.evidence: Allowable values are specified for earth leakage current, touch current, patient leakage current, and patient auxiliary current.

evidence.claim: Protective earth connections forming a means of operator protection must comply with IEC 60601-1 clause 8.6 or IEC 60950-1 protective earthing requirements and tests.
evidence.evidenceText: Protective earth connections forming a means of operator protection shall either comply with 8.6 or comply with IEC 60950-1 for protective earthing.

evidence.claim: Y2 capacitors complying with IEC 60384-14 may count as one means of operator protection if they pass the corresponding dielectric strength test.
evidence.evidenceText: A Y2 capacitor complying with IEC 60384-14 is considered equivalent to one means of operator protection if it passes the dielectric strength test for one means.

evidence.claim: Y1 capacitors complying with IEC 60384-14 may count as two means of operator protection if they pass the corresponding dielectric strength test.
evidence.evidenceText: A Y1 capacitor complying with IEC 60384-14 is considered equivalent to two means of operator protection if it passes the dielectric strength test for two means.

evidence.claim: F-type applied part patient connections must be separated from all other parts by one means of patient protection for a working voltage equal to maximum mains voltage.
evidence.evidenceText: Patient connections of any F-type applied part shall be separated from all other parts by means equivalent to one means of patient protection for working voltage equal to maximum mains voltage.

evidence.claim: F-type applied parts must meet the patient leakage current limit with 110% of maximum mains voltage applied.
evidence.evidenceText: F-type applied part separation shall comply with the specified limit for patient leakage current with 110% of the maximum mains voltage applied.

evidence.claim: Type BF, Type CF, and defibrillation-proof classification applies to the whole of one applied part.
evidence.evidenceText: The classification as Type BF, Type CF or defibrillation-proof applies to the whole of one applied part.

evidence.claim: Type B applied part patient connections that are not protectively earthed require one means of patient protection from unearthed metal accessible parts unless risk-based exceptions are met.
evidence.evidenceText: Patient connections of a Type B applied part not protectively earthed shall be separated by one means of patient protection from metal accessible parts not protectively earthed, unless stated low-risk conditions apply.

evidence.claim: Patient lead connectors with conductive parts not separated by one means of patient protection must be constructed to prevent connection to earth or hazardous voltage while contacting the patient.
evidence.evidenceText: Such a connector shall be constructed so the conductive part cannot become connected to earth or possible hazardous voltage while patient connections contact the patient.

evidence.claim: Maximum mains voltage is 250 V for other internally powered ME equipment.
evidence.evidenceText: For other internally powered ME equipment, the maximum mains voltage is 250 V.

evidence.claim: Working voltage for a means of protection may be determined by inspection, calculation, or measurement.
evidence.evidenceText: The working voltage is determined by inspection, calculation or measurement, according to 8.5.4.

evidence.claim: For defibrillation-proof applied parts, working voltage is determined without considering possible defibrillation voltages.
evidence.evidenceText: For defibrillation-proof applied parts, the working voltage is determined without regard to the possible presence of defibrillation voltages.

evidence.claim: Defibrillation-proof applied parts must prevent hazardous electrical energies above 1 V peak from appearing on enclosure, signal input/output parts, test foil, or other applied-part patient connections during defibrillator discharge.
evidence.evidenceText: During cardiac defibrillator discharge, hazardous electrical energies exceeding 1 V peak between Y1 and Y2 shall not appear on listed enclosure, signal, foil, or other patient connection locations.

evidence.claim: After defibrillation voltage exposure and any documented recovery time, ME equipment must still provide basic safety and essential performance.
evidence.evidenceText: Following exposure to defibrillation voltage and any recovery time stated in accompanying documents, ME equipment shall comply with relevant requirements and continue to provide basic safety and essential performance.

evidence.claim: Defibrillation-proof applied parts must deliver at least 90% of the defibrillator energy to a 100 ohm load compared with the equipment disconnected.
evidence.evidenceText: Defibrillation-proof applied parts or patient connections shall incorporate means so energy delivered to a 100 ohm load is at least 90% of the energy with ME equipment disconnected.

evidence.claim: Protective earth requirements in clauses 8.6.2 through 8.6.8 do not apply where parts comply with IEC 60950-1 protective earthing and serve only as means of operator protection.
evidence.evidenceText: Requirements of 8.6.2 to 8.6.8 apply unless parts comply with IEC 60950-1 protective earthing and serve as means of operator protection but not patient protection.

evidence.claim: For permanently installed ME equipment and ME equipment with an appliance inlet, protective earth impedance must not exceed 100 milliohms except as allowed by 8.6.4 b).
evidence.evidenceText: Impedance between the protective earth terminal or appliance inlet earth pin and any protectively earthed part shall not exceed 100 mΩ, except as allowed by 8.6.4 b).

evidence.claim: For ME equipment with a non-detachable power supply cord, impedance between the mains plug protective earth pin and any protectively earthed part must not exceed 200 milliohms except as allowed by 8.6.4 b).
evidence.evidenceText: For ME equipment with a non-detachable power supply cord, impedance between the protective earth pin in the mains plug and any protectively earthed part shall not exceed 200 mΩ.

evidence.claim: Protective earth impedance testing uses 25 A or 1.5 times the highest rated current, whichever is greater, at 50 Hz or 60 Hz for 5 s to 10 s.
evidence.evidenceText: A current of 25 A or 1.5 times the highest rated current, whichever is greater, from a 50 Hz or 60 Hz source is passed for 5 s to 10 s.

evidence.claim: Potential equalization conductor terminals must be operator-accessible, minimize accidental disconnection, detach without a tool, not be used for protective earth, and be marked with IEC 60417-5021.
evidence.evidenceText: The terminal shall be accessible to the operator, minimize accidental disconnection, allow detachment without a tool, not be used for protective earth, and be marked with IEC 60417-5021.

evidence.claim: Class II ME equipment with isolated internal screens and a three-conductor power cord may use the third conductor only as functional earth for those screens.
evidence.evidenceText: If Class II ME equipment with isolated internal screens has a three-conductor power cord, the third conductor shall be used only as the functional earth connection for these screens.

evidence.claim: Leakage current limits apply at operating temperature, after humidity preconditioning, in normal and specified single fault conditions, with highest rated supply frequency, and with 110% highest rated mains voltage.
evidence.evidenceText: Specified earth leakage, touch, patient leakage, and patient auxiliary current values apply under combinations including operating temperature, humidity preconditioning, NC/SFC, highest rated frequency, and 110% highest rated mains voltage.

evidence.claim: Touch current allowable values are 100 microamps in normal condition and 500 microamps in single fault condition.
evidence.evidenceText: The allowable values of the touch current are 100 μA in normal condition and 500 μA in single fault condition.

evidence.claim: Earth leakage current allowable values are 5 mA in normal condition and 10 mA in single fault condition, with higher values allowed for certain permanently installed ME equipment.
evidence.evidenceText: Earth leakage current values are 5 mA in normal condition and 10 mA in single fault condition; a higher value is allowed for permanently installed ME equipment connected to a dedicated supply circuit.

evidence.claim: No leakage current may exceed 10 mA rms in normal or single fault condition when measured with a non-frequency-weighted device.
evidence.evidenceText: Regardless of waveform and frequency, no leakage current shall exceed 10 mA r.m.s. in normal condition or single fault condition when measured with a non-frequency-weighted device.

evidence.claim: Patient leakage current under special test conditions for an F-type applied part caused by external voltage on the patient connection is 5000 microamps for Type BF and 50 microamps for Type CF.
evidence.evidenceText: Table 4 lists patient leakage current caused by external voltage on an F-type applied part patient connection: Type BF 5,000 μA and Type CF 50 μA; Type B not applicable.

evidence.claim: Leakage current measurements close to allowed limits or of doubtful validity must use the applicable IEC test figure as the deciding factor.
evidence.evidenceText: Other test figures may yield accurate results, but if results are close to allowed values or validity is doubtful, the applicable test figure is used as the deciding factor.

reviewFlags.reason: This is chunk 5 of 20 and contains only clauses 8.5 to early 8.7.4.7; document-level title and edition details are inferred from page headers and source path.
